evidence_summary: Both are interferons in the same signaling family. IFN-beta has cell-type-specific and context-dependent effects: in monocytes it has been shown to modulate inflammatory responses, while in CD4+ T cells it can suppress IFN-gamma. Direct induction of IFN-gamma by IFN-beta in monocytes is not the canonical documented cascade; co-regulation in the type I/II IFN axis is well established.
citations: Interferon-Beta Induces Distinct Gene Expression Response Patterns in Human Monocytes versus T cells (PLOS One) — https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0062366
Salmonella Induced IL-23 and IL-1β Allow for IL-12 Production by Monocytes... through Induction of IFN-γ — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2791865/
search_queries_used: IFN-beta induces IFN-gamma production monocytes

evidence_summary: IL-2 is well-established to induce IFN-gamma production in CD8+ T cells via STAT5/T-bet signaling, with multiple papers showing autocrine and paracrine IL-2 amplifies IFN-gamma and granzyme B output in CD8+ effector and memory T cells.
citations: IL-2 Produced by CD8+ Immune T cells Can Augment Their IFN-γ Production — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3578122/
CD4+ T Cell-Derived IL-2 Signals during Early Priming Advances Primary CD8+ T Cell Responses — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2770320/
search_queries_used: IL-2 induces IFN-gamma CD8 T cells

evidence_summary: Decorin has been linked to IL-8 modulation in cancer/macrophage settings, with one study showing reduced decorin increases IL-8 in macrophages and another showing decorin-silenced oral cancer cells lose IL-8 expression. Effects are context-dependent and there is no clear direct A→B cascade documented in immune cells, particularly not in CD8 T cells.
citations: A role for aberrantly expressed nuclear localized decorin in migration and invasion of dysplastic and malignant oral epithelial cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3198745/
Decorin Secreted by Human Umbilical Cord Blood-Derived Mesenchymal Stem Cells Induces Macrophage Polarization via CD44 — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6801980/
search_queries_used: Decorin induces IL-8 production immune cells

evidence_summary: IL-6 directly induces decorin mRNA in human endothelial cells in a dose-dependent manner (max at 10 ng/ml), although translation requires extracellular collagen signals. This is a well-documented direct induction of B by A, though not specifically in CD8 T cells.
citations: Interleukin (IL)-6 and IL-10 induce decorin mRNA in endothelial cells — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15016829/
search_queries_used: IL-6 induces Decorin expression

evidence_summary: TNF-alpha potently promotes Th9 cell differentiation and IL-9 production through TNFR2-dependent pathways. iTregs and Foxp3+ regulatory T cells are among the cell populations producing IL-9 downstream of TNF-alpha signaling.
citations: TNF-α enhances Th9 cell differentiation and antitumor immunity via TNFR2-dependent pathways — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6360681/
Transcription factor Foxo1 is essential for IL-9 induction in T helper cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5634439/
search_queries_used: TNF-alpha induces IL-9 production T regulatory cells

evidence_summary: The direction is reversed in the literature: IL-27 INHIBITS VEGF expression via STAT1 signaling and has documented anti-angiogenic properties. Both molecules are in the same axis (inflammation-angiogenesis) but A→B induction is not supported; the inverse is documented.
citations: IL-27 inhibits epithelial-mesenchymal transition and angiogenic factor production in a STAT1-dominant pathway in human non-small cell lung cancer — https://jeccr.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/1756-9966-32-97
search_queries_used: IL-27 induces VEGF expression

evidence_summary: GM-CSF is used to differentiate monocytes/DCs that can subsequently produce TSLP in response to LPS or other stimuli, and GM-CSF/TSLP/STAT3 signaling has been linked in cancer contexts. However direct GM-CSF→TSLP induction in immune cells (especially NKT) is not clearly established as a documented cascade.
citations: Thymic Stromal Lymphopoietin is Produced by Dendritic Cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3140600/
search_queries_used: GM-CSF induces TSLP production

evidence_summary: IL-17A drives IL-36 agonist production (IL-36α/β/γ) in keratinocytes, and the IL-17A/IL-36 axis is well-established in psoriasis. IL-36Ra (the antagonist) is part of the same regulatory circuit and IL-38 (a related antagonist) is upregulated by anti-IL-17A treatment, suggesting feedback regulation rather than direct A→B induction in NKT cells.
citations: IL-17A-driven psoriasis is critically dependent on IL-36 signaling — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10754973/
The psoriasis-associated IL-17A induces and cooperates with IL-36 cytokines — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5688102/
search_queries_used: IL-17A induces IL-36 receptor antagonist expression

evidence_summary: No clear literature identified linking IL-9 to APRIL induction in any cell type, let alone CD8 T cells. APRIL is primarily produced by myeloid cells in response to TLR agonists and inflammation, not via documented IL-9 signaling.
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces APRIL TNFSF13 expression B cells
IL-9 induces APRIL plasma cells

evidence_summary: While CD30L is expressed on monocytes and can mediate various pleiotropic effects, no clear literature was found establishing CD30L→VEGF induction in CD14+ monocytes or any other immune cell type.
search_queries_used: CD30L TNFSF8 induces VEGF monocytes angiogenesis

evidence_summary: Decorin modulates macrophage polarization (M1/M2) and influences cytokine output in mesenchymal stem cells, but no direct evidence was found linking decorin to IL-27 induction, especially in MAIT cells.
search_queries_used: Decorin induces IL-27 expression macrophages

evidence_summary: APRIL is known to regulate B/T cell responses (collagen arthritis, IL-17, Th2 responses) but no documented evidence of APRIL inducing IL-27 production in immune cells, particularly MAIT cells.
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 induces IL-27 expression

evidence_summary: APRIL primarily acts on B cell survival, plasma cell maintenance, and IgA regulation. No documented evidence found linking APRIL to FasL induction in CD4 T cells or other immune contexts.
search_queries_used: APRIL induces FasL expression T cells apoptosis
APRIL induces Fas FasL B cells lymphoma

evidence_summary: No literature found connecting APRIL/TNFSF13 to EGF (epidermal growth factor) induction in T cells or immune cells. These molecules operate in distinct signaling families with no documented cascade.
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 EGF epidermal growth factor T cells

evidence_summary: IL-36Ra is the natural antagonist of IL-36 agonists, which themselves induce VEGF and angiogenesis. IL-36Ra INHIBITS VEGF expression by blocking IL-36 signaling — opposite direction of A→B induction. The molecules are coupled via the IL-36/VEGF axis.
citations: IL-36γ Augments Ocular Angiogenesis by Promoting the VEGF–VEGFR Axis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10616713/
IL-36γ Is a Strong Inducer of IL-23 in Psoriatic Cells and Activates Angiogenesis — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5834930/
search_queries_used: IL-36Ra induces VEGF expression angiogenesis

evidence_summary: TL1A/TNFSF15 drives IL-13 production primarily from ILC2s and Th2/Th9 cells (well-documented in muco-secretory fibrotic disorders and gut inflammation). The relay cell type in this case is CD14 Mono, where monocytes/macrophages are the primary SOURCE of TL1A, not the producer of IL-13 — so the documented cascade goes via lymphocytes, not directly through monocytes.
citations: Targeting TL1A/DR3 Signaling Offers a Therapeutic Advantage to Neutralizing IL13/IL4Rα — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8299868/
The tale of TL1A in inflammation — https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201120
search_queries_used: TL1A TNFSF15 induces IL-13 production monocytes

evidence_summary: No clear evidence of IL-16 inducing VEGF expression in immune cells, particularly MAIT cells. Multiple other interleukins (IL-6, IL-17, IL-1) are well-documented VEGF inducers, but IL-16 is not.
search_queries_used: IL-16 induces VEGF expression angiogenesis

evidence_summary: CD70/CD27L is upregulated upon T/B cell activation, and IFN-gamma can induce CD70 on T cells. No documented evidence of APRIL inducing CD70/CD27L expression in NKT cells or other immune cells.
search_queries_used: APRIL induces CD70 CD27L expression T cells

evidence_summary: CD70/CD27L signals through CD27 leading to NF-kB/c-Jun activation, T cell costimulation and survival. No documented direct link to VEGF induction in MAIT cells or other immune cell types in the literature found.
search_queries_used: CD70 CD27L induces VEGF angiogenesis

evidence_summary: IL-12 is documented as ANTI-angiogenic (opposite direction to A→B), acting via IFN-γ/IP-10 to inhibit neovascularization and VEGF-dependent angiogenesis. Both molecules are coupled in the angiogenesis axis but the documented cascade is IL-12 inhibiting (not inducing) VEGF.
citations: IL-12 Inhibition of Endothelial Cell Functions and Angiogenesis — https://journals.aai.org/jimmunol/article/166/6/3890/70412/
search_queries_used: IL-12 induces VEGF monocytes angiogenesis
